message:
feat(middleware): add response timing metrics collection (fastapi_rust-lom)

Implements comprehensive timing metrics for monitoring and observability:

- ServerTimingEntry: represents a single timing metric with optional description
- ServerTimingBuilder: builds Server-Timing headers with multiple metrics
- TimingMetrics: collected timing data stored in request extensions
  - Custom metrics (db, cache, etc.) can be added by handlers
  - Time-to-first-byte (TTFB) tracking
  - Automatic total request duration
- TimingMetricsConfig: configurable timing options
  - Production mode (minimal headers)
  - Development mode (full timing details)
- TimingMetricsMiddleware: middleware that:
  - Injects TimingMetrics into request extensions
  - Adds X-Response-Time header
  - Adds Server-Timing header with all metrics
- TimingHistogram: Prometheus-style histogram buckets for aggregation
  - HTTP latency presets
  - Count, sum, mean calculations

Includes 17 unit tests covering all functionality.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

 {"id":"bd-3ihr","title":"Router: Wildcard catch-all route support ({*path})","description":"Router doesn't support catch-all wildcard routes like '/files/{*path}' that match any remaining path segments. Essential for file serving and SPA routing.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-28T02:51:29.208480863Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-28T04:38:52.795218911Z","closed_at":"2026-01-28T04:38:52.795153479Z","close_reason":"Implemented {*path} wildcard syntax for catch-all routes. Added 13 comprehensive tests covering file serving, SPA routing, priority, conflicts, and syntax equivalence.","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-3ihr","depends_on_id":"bd-fhmy","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-28T02:56:04.030031370Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}],"comments":[{"id":9,"issue_id":"bd-3ihr","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"## Background & Context
Wildcard/catch-all routes like `/files/{*path}` match any remaining path segments. Essential for:
- Static file serving: `/static/{*filepath}` matches `/static/js/app.js`
- SPA routing: `/{*route}` catches all frontend routes for client-side router
- Proxy routes: `/api/{*rest}` forwards to backend

## Current Limitation
Router only supports single-segment parameters `{id}`. No way to match multiple segments.

## Solution Architecture

### Route Pattern Syntax
```
/files/{*path}     -> matches /files/a, /files/a/b, /files/a/b/c
/api/v1/{*rest}    -> captures everything after /api/v1/
```

### Trie Implementation
Wildcard nodes are special leaf nodes that match any remaining path:
```rust
enum TrieNode {
    Static { segment: String, children: Vec<TrieNode> },
    Param { name: String, children: Vec<TrieNode> },
    Wildcard { name: String, route: Route },  // Always a leaf
}
```

### Matching Priority
1. Static segments (exact match)
2. Single-segment parameters
3. Wildcard catch-all (lowest priority)

### Captured Value
Wildcard captures entire remaining path:
```rust
// Route: /files/{*path}
// Request: /files/css/styles/main.css
// Captured: path = "css/styles/main.css"
```

## Files to Modify
- crates/fastapi-router/src/lib.rs - Trie and Router
- crates/fastapi-router/src/trie.rs (if separate)

## Dependencies
- Should coordinate with bd-5bt0 (route priority rules)
- Should update bd-2b8n (type converters) - wildcard is always String

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] `{*name}` syntax recognized in route patterns
- [ ] Wildcard matches zero or more segments
- [ ] Captured value is complete remaining path
- [ ] Wildcard has lowest priority (static > param > wildcard)
- [ ] Only one wildcard per route (at end)
- [ ] Tests cover edge cases

 {"id":"bd-3jgt","title":"CRITICAL: Fix rich_rust dependency path in fastapi-output","description":"The rich_rust dependency in fastapi-output/Cargo.toml uses a broken path '../../../rich_rust' which doesn't exist. This blocks the entire rich output feature from compiling.","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-28T02:48:58.676457053Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-28T03:15:45.315586191Z","closed_at":"2026-01-28T03:15:38.361563448Z","close_reason":"Fixed rich_rust dependency - using git dependency, re-enabled fastapi-output in workspace, fixed compile errors in routing_debug.rs","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-3jgt","depends_on_id":"bd-nww8","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-28T02:56:40.385854229Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}],"comments":[{"id":4,"issue_id":"bd-3jgt","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"## Background & Context
The fastapi-output crate provides rich terminal output for development (colored logs, ASCII banners, styled errors). It depends on rich_rust for the terminal rendering capabilities.

## Current Problem
In crates/fastapi-output/Cargo.toml:
```toml
[dependencies.rich_rust]
path = "../../../rich_rust"  # This path doesn't exist!
optional = true
```

The path assumes rich_rust is a sibling of the fastapi_rust workspace root, but:
1. rich_rust may not be checked out at that location
2. This breaks `cargo build` for the entire workspace when the feature is enabled
3. CI/CD pipelines will fail

## Impact
- `cargo build -p fastapi-output --features rich` fails
- Cannot test or use rich output features
- Blocks Phase 2+ of rich output integration
- Makes fastapi-output effectively unusable

## Solution Options

### Option 1: Use crates.io (Recommended if published)
```toml
[dependencies]
rich_rust = { version = "0.1", optional = true }
```
Pros: Standard, portable, versioned
Cons: Requires rich_rust to be published

### Option 2: Git dependency
```toml
[dependencies.rich_rust]
git = "https://github.com/user/rich_rust"
optional = true
```
Pros: Works before crates.io publish
Cons: Slower builds, no offline

### Option 3: Workspace member
Move rich_rust into this workspace as a crate
Pros: Full control, workspace optimization
Cons: Bloats this repo if rich_rust is separate project

### Option 4: Conditional path with fallback
```toml
[target.'cfg(feature = "rich-local")'.dependencies]
rich_rust = { path = "../rich_rust" }
```
For development only

## Recommendation
1. Short-term: Option 2 (git dependency) to unblock development
2. Long-term: Option 1 (crates.io) when rich_rust is published

## Files to Modify
- crates/fastapi-output/Cargo.toml

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] `cargo build -p fastapi-output` succeeds
- [ ] `cargo build -p fastapi-output --features rich` succeeds (if feature exists)
- [ ] CI passes
- [ ] Document the dependency strategy in README

## Dependencies
- Requires rich_rust crate to be available (published or git)

## Considerations
- rich_rust is Dan's custom crate - need to verify it's ready for external use
- May need to fork/vendor if not available
- Consider making rich optional with fallback to plain output

All tests pass (19 total).","created_at":"2026-01-28T03:15:45Z"}]}
 {"id":"bd-3kvs","title":"Router: Document unsafe code blocks","description":"Router trie implementation has unsafe code blocks without safety documentation. Each unsafe block needs SAFETY comments explaining invariants and why it's sound.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-28T02:51:32.881125074Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-28T04:49:14.844380476Z","closed_at":"2026-01-28T04:49:14.844316998Z","close_reason":"Added comprehensive SAFETY documentation to all unsafe code blocks in registry.rs. Documented: (1) registered_routes() - explains the safety contract with registrations(), (2) registrations() for Linux/ELF - explains linker section invariants, symbol guarantees, and slice safety, (3) registrations() for non-ELF - documents the safe empty fallback, (4) static anchor - explains why the link_section attribute is safe.","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-3kvs","depends_on_id":"bd-fhmy","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-28T02:56:04.803426166Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"bd-3kxd","title":"CRITICAL: Route struct missing handler fn pointer","description":"The Route struct in fastapi-router is missing the handler function pointer field, blocking all handler dispatch. Routes can match but cannot execute handlers. This is THE most critical gap in the entire framework.","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-28T02:48:54.887496197Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-28T03:22:58.552620569Z","closed_at":"2026-01-28T03:22:58.552556900Z","close_reason":"Implemented handler field in Route struct. Added Handler import, Arc<dyn Handler> field, custom Debug impl, Route::new() with handler param, with_arc_handler(), handler() getter, PlaceholderHandler for macros. All 59 router tests pass.","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-3kxd","depends_on_id":"bd-229o","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-28T02:59:31.910506135Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-3kxd","depends_on_id":"bd-fhmy","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-28T02:56:03.014972651Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}],"comments":[{"id":3,"issue_id":"bd-3kxd","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"## Background & Context
The Route struct in fastapi-router/src/lib.rs currently stores route metadata (method, pattern, converter, path) but is missing the most essential field - the handler function pointer that should be invoked when a route matches.

## Current Implementation Gap
```rust
pub struct Route {
    pub method: Method,
    pub pattern: String,
    pub converter: Option<&'static str>,
    pub path: String,
    // MISSING: handler field!
}
```

## Why This Is Critical
- Routes can be registered and matched, but there's no way to dispatch to handlers
- The Router.lookup() returns Option<&Route> but Route contains no handler
- This makes the entire framework non-functional for its primary purpose
- ALL other features depend on this working (extractors, middleware, responses)

## Solution Architecture
The handler field should store a type-erased async function. Options considered:

### Option 1: Box<dyn Fn> (Recommended)
```rust
type Handler = Box<dyn Fn(&Cx, Request) -> Pin<Box<dyn Future<Output = Response> + Send>> + Send + Sync>;
```
Pros: Simple, works with closures
Cons: Double indirection, heap allocation per call

### Option 2: Arc<dyn HandlerTrait>
```rust
trait Handler: Send + Sync {
    fn call(&self, cx: &Cx, req: Request) -> Pin<Box<dyn Future<Output = Response> + Send>>;
}
```
Pros: Allows shared handlers, extensible
Cons: More complexity

### Option 3: Generic Handler<H>
Pros: Zero-cost, monomorphized
Cons: Requires type erasure at registration anyway

## Recommendation
Use Option 1 with a Handler type alias. Simple, works, can optimize later.

## Files to Modify
1. crates/fastapi-router/src/lib.rs - Add handler field to Route, update Router
2. May need Handler trait in fastapi-core for reuse

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Route struct has handler field
- [ ] Router::add() accepts handler parameter
- [ ] Router::lookup() returns route with callable handler
- [ ] Handler works with async functions via Cx
- [ ] All existing router tests pass
- [ ] New tests verify handler dispatch

## Dependencies
None - this is foundational

## Estimated Complexity
Medium - straightforward addition but needs careful async handling

 {"id":"fastapi_rust-k9h","title":"Request timeout via Budget","description":"Use asupersync's Budget for request timeouts:

## Budget Configuration
- Default budget per request (configurable)
- Budget propagates through handler and dependencies
- Remaining budget accessible via RequestContext.budget()

## Timeout Behavior
- Budget exhaustion triggers cancellation
- Handler receives CancelledError at next checkpoint
- Response: 504 Gateway Timeout with configurable body
- Clean resource cleanup via structured concurrency

## Integration Points
- TCP server creates budget for each request
- Middleware chain respects budget
- Extractors check budget before expensive operations
- Dependencies can subdivide budget

## Test Requirements
- Unit: Budget creation and propagation
- Unit: Timeout triggers cancellation
- Integration: Slow handler returns 504
- Integration: Cleanup runs on timeout","acceptance_criteria":"Slow handlers cancelled when budget exhausted, return 504.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"","owner":"jeff141421@gmail.com","estimated_minutes":90,"created_at":"2026-01-17T14:08:09.176106485Z","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","updated_at":"2026-01-21T11:09:19.659784397Z","closed_at":"2026-01-21T11:09:19.659738230Z","close_reason":"Implemented request timeout via Budget API:
- Added Cx::for_testing_with_budget(budget: Budget) to asupersync for creating Cx with custom budget
- Wired ServerConfig request_timeout to Budget deadline in server.rs
- Added timeout detection after handler completion using Instant elapsed time
- Returns 504 Gateway Timeout when request exceeds configured timeout
- Added 6 unit tests for timeout behavior (budget creation, duration conversion, 504 response)","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["asupersync","cross-cutting","timeout"]}
 {"id":"fastapi_rust-kl2","title":"Checkpoint integration in extractors and middleware","description":"Integrate cancellation checkpoints throughout the request pipeline for cancel-correctness. Checkpoint locations: before body read, after body parse, before each dependency resolve, before handler call, in middleware pre/post. Use ctx.checkpoint() to allow graceful cancellation. Masked sections: transaction commits, response send. Benefits: early exit on client disconnect, clean resource cleanup. Test: verify checkpoint returns CancelledError when cancelled, masked sections complete, resources cleaned up.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"","owner":"jeff141421@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-01-17T15:45:43.727662665Z","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","updated_at":"2026-01-23T07:31:39.003866055Z","closed_at":"2026-01-23T07:31:39.003810070Z","close_reason":"Completed checkpoint integration + tests","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["asupersync","cancel","extractors","middleware"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"fastapi_rust-kl2","depends_on_id":"fastapi_rust-3f1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-28T00:10:33Z","created_by":"import"}]}
 {"id":"fastapi_rust-kpe","title":"Scope constraint validation","description":"Enforce dependency scope constraints:

RULE (per FastAPI spec):
Request-scoped dependency CANNOT depend on function-scoped dependency.
This would cause the request-scoped to hold stale function-scoped value.

EXAMPLE VIOLATION:
@depends(scope='request')
async fn get_cached_user(db: Depends<DbConn, scope='function'>) -> User
// ERROR: request scope can't depend on function scope

VALIDATION:
- Check at dependency graph construction
- Raise DependencyScopeError
- Clear message explaining the violation

ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA:
- Scope violation detected at startup
- Clear error message
- Explain why it's invalid
- Allow valid scope combinations

TEST REQUIREMENTS:
- Unit: Invalid scope combination
- Unit: Valid scope combinations
- Unit: Nested scope validation","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"","owner":"jeff141421@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-01-17T15:12:18.297715683Z","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","updated_at":"2026-01-28T23:13:09.051542007Z","closed_at":"2026-01-28T23:13:09.051409270Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["di","phase-4","safety"]}

 {"id":"fastapi_rust-lrz","title":"Dependency overrides for testing","description":"Implement dependency override system for testing:

API:
app.dependency_overrides[RealDbPool] = MockDbPool
app.dependency_overrides.insert::<RealDbPool>(mock_pool);

BEHAVIOR:
- When resolving Depends<RealDbPool>, use MockDbPool instead
- Override checked before normal resolution
- Supports any FromRequest type
- Clear overrides: app.dependency_overrides.clear()

USE IN TESTS:
#[test]
fn test_with_mock() {
    let mut app = create_app();
    app.override_dependency::<DbPool>(mock_pool);
    let client = TestClient::new(app);
    // Tests use mock_pool instead of real pool
}

ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA:
- Override replaces real dependency
- Type-safe override API
- Works with nested dependencies
- Clear/reset functionality

TEST REQUIREMENTS:
- Unit: Override application
- Unit: Nested override
- Unit: Clear overrides
- Integration: Test client with overrides","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"","owner":"jeff141421@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-01-17T15:11:43.912622237Z","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","updated_at":"2026-01-21T10:25:25.344555165Z","closed_at":"2026-01-21T10:25:25.344475676Z","close_reason":"Implementation complete: DependencyOverrides provides insert(), insert_value(), clear(), and resolve() methods. Tests verify override substitution, nested resolution, complex graphs, and clear functionality. Integration with TestClient working.","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["di","phase-4","testing"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"fastapi_rust-lrz","depends_on_id":"fastapi_rust-aha","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-28T00:10:33Z","created_by":"import"}]}

 {"id":"fastapi_rust-n12","title":"Graceful shutdown with structured concurrency","description":"Graceful shutdown using asupersync's structured concurrency:

## Shutdown Phases
1. Stop accepting new connections
2. Set shutdown flag (new requests get 503)
3. Wait for in-flight requests (with grace period)
4. Cancel remaining requests after grace period
5. Run shutdown hooks
6. Close server region

## Signal Handling
- SIGTERM/SIGINT triggers shutdown
- Second signal forces immediate exit
- Configurable signals

## Grace Period
- Default: 30 seconds
- Requests get remaining budget subdivided
- Requests exceeding grace get cancelled

## Region Closing
- Connection regions closed when idle
- Request tasks cancelled with CancelReason::shutdown
- Cleanup handlers run for all regions

## Test Requirements
- Unit: Shutdown signal handling
- Unit: Grace period enforcement
- Integration: In-flight requests complete
- Integration: Stuck requests cancelled","acceptance_criteria":"Server shutdown waits for requests with budget, then cancels remaining.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","owner":"jeff141421@gmail.com","estimated_minutes":120,"created_at":"2026-01-17T14:08:10.222632170Z","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","updated_at":"2026-01-19T02:32:19.369216312Z","closed_at":"2026-01-18T17:23:09.304511511Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["asupersync","cross-cutting","shutdown"]}
